text.title: Stripe Payment Links No Backend Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every payment-links-spec MUST enforce. Hard rules — operator input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every webhook handler (Zapier code-step, Make scenario, or serverless function) MUST verify the Stripe-Signature header using HMAC-SHA256 with the endpoint's signing secret before acting on the payload.
rule.rationale: Unverified payloads let an attacker forge `checkout.session.completed` and trigger free fulfillment. Stripe ships a signature header for this reason; default tolerance is 5 minutes, libraries enforce it.

rule.statement: The webhook integration MUST preserve the raw request body byte-for-byte before any JSON parse or framework middleware touches it. Signature verification operates on the raw bytes, not on a re-serialized JSON.
rule.rationale: Any framework that pretty-prints, re-orders keys, or normalizes whitespace breaks HMAC verification. Stripe explicitly documents this as the #1 webhook-rejection failure mode.

rule.statement: Every fulfillment action MUST be guarded by an idempotency key derived from `event.id` (the Stripe event ID). The integration MUST consult a persistent store (Sheets row, KV, DB row) before running side effects.
rule.rationale: Stripe retries webhook delivery with exponential backoff for up to 3 days. Without idempotency, a single payment fires 5–10 fulfillment emails / license keys / Discord pings.

rule.statement: The payment-links-spec MUST name a single accountable owner (handle / email / org role with on-call rotation). "team", "support@", or "we" are rejected.
rule.rationale: Live money flows demand a human throat to choke when a webhook silently fails. Plural ownership means nobody pages on a 4xx response from the fulfillment step.

rule.statement: The spec MUST carry semver and `last_reviewed` ISO date. Records older than 90 days without review MUST be flagged on read; secrets MUST be rotated annually or on suspected compromise.
rule.rationale: Stripe recommends rotating webhook endpoint secrets periodically. A frozen 1.0.0 spec with stale secrets is a leak waiting to happen.

rule.statement: When `billing_model == one-off` AND `custom_amount_per_buyer == false` AND `connect_required == false`, the spec MUST select Stripe Payment Links (hosted URL) and document one webhook endpoint per fulfillment channel.
rule.rationale: Payment Links are purpose-built for fixed-price digital sales; using Checkout Session here adds backend overhead without value.

rule.statement: When `custom_amount_per_buyer == true` OR `dynamic_metadata == true`, the spec MUST escalate to Stripe Checkout Session (still no backend if Zapier Stripe action creates the session) and forbid raw Payment Link reuse.
rule.rationale: Payment Links cannot accept buyer-supplied amounts; Checkout Session can. Misusing a Payment Link for variable pricing leads to manual refund/regenerate cycles.

rule.statement: When `connect_required == true` (marketplace, application_fee, transfer_data), the spec MUST reject the no-backend pattern and surface a recommendation to load `stripe-connect-backend` (not in this methodology).
rule.rationale: Payment Links do not support `transfer_data` or `application_fee_amount`. Forcing Connect through Zapier produces undefined behavior and PCI exposure.
